change: Eliminated redundant per-step host work in native c>1 decode: (1) cache the static (rows,slots) block table in _batch_full_spans (skip host build + synchronous host->device copy every full-attention layer every step; invalidate on prefill writes + reset), (2) persistent device token scratch in _set_batch_token_embeddings (no malloc/free per step), (3) persistent device position scratch in _set_batch_positions.

numerically_identical_evidence.c2_batched_lm_head_intermittent: pre-existing documented batched-sampler race (after reps eq_prefix 0/137/104; row0 emitted row1's sequence). Not caused by this change; serial_lm_head is stable.

note: c1 unchanged (+0.4%, the c=1 path does not use the batch setters/_batch_full_spans). Native c>1 decode improves c2 +14.7% (batched) / c4 +15.9% / c8 +19.9%. Still correctness-blocked for retained claims (free-running equality is chaos-limited; teacher-forced KL/top-1 is the correctness gate and is unchanged since tokens are byte-identical). No retained scoreboard row.

commands.after: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_retained_bench.py --model <MODEL> --fixture <FIXTURE> --prompt-length 512 --batch-size {2,4,8} --decode-tokens 128 --warmup-decode-tokens 8 --max-layers 40 --batch-decode-full-attn-path native_batch --compiler-version-file /tmp/hipengine-retained/hipcc-version.txt --require-cached-build --json ...; c1 via scripts/qwen35_paro_bench.py
